title: The Modern Data Scientist: A Roadmap for the Age of AI Agents
summary: The job hasn't disappeared. The mental model has changed. A practitioner's guide to the tool stack, skill demand, and learning roadmap for data scientists and engineers in the AI agent era — with a South African lens.

title: How I Learned to Build My Own Python Libraries (From Curiosity to Real Work)
summary: A practical guide to designing, packaging, and publishing Python libraries from first principles.

title: Ubunye Engine: One Framework, One Convention, One CLI — Why Unity Is the Real Deliverable
summary: I built a production-grade ML/ETL framework — config system, lineage tracking, model registry, CI/CD, documentation, 261 tests — in approximately 30 human hours using an AI coding agent, at a total cost of R1,520. This is the unfiltered account: the CI failures at 2am, the bug in a three-line fallback, the API hallucinations, and the real economics of building with an agent versus a team. The central argument is simple: the discipline to ship boring work is rarer than the ability to design interesting architecture, and it matters more
